note: The one events file. The app sorts future events to the top and moves past ones into the archive automatically, so this page can never go stale. Admin > Events can draft entries and download a merged copy of this file to publish. The archive below is real history imported from the WordPress export.

events.title: FCDC Annual Meeting & Conference: Next Edition
events.body: The Faith Community Development Corporation's annual gathering of congregations, municipalities, and funders. Organizations may register to attend or join as a paid sponsor. Sponsors get the stage, the program book, and the room. (Sample date; set the real one in data/events.json.)

events.title: Equity Uprise Summit: Standing Against the Monopoles

press.title: Bridgeport Church at Risk From UI's Monopoles Project
The monopole fight in Bridgeport and Fairfield
Bridgeport and Fairfield officials applaud governor's support on monopole fight
UI asks regulators to take another look at proposed power line

events.body: Anchored by Pastor Carl McCluster, 85-year-old Shiloh Baptist became ground zero for the community's stand against United Illuminating's Docket 516R plan to run high-voltage transmission monopoles through Bridgeport and Fairfield's residential and historic districts, threatening Shiloh itself and the historic Mary & Eliza Freeman Houses. Residents and clergy were joined by a bipartisan delegation including Senator Tony Hwang, Representative Jennifer Leeper, city council members, and Freeman Center president Maisa Tisdale, raising transparency and environmental-justice concerns the process had ignored. That October, the Connecticut Siting Council denied UI's application, and the community's unified front was recognized as a major factor in the rejection.

events.title: FCDC Annual Meeting & Conference: "Defining Light in This Present Darkness of Gun Violence"
events.body: Faith CDC's annual meeting and conference workshops: empowering, educating, and equipping people and communities in response to gun violence.

events.title: "United We Stand": Connecticut for the People of Ukraine
events.body: Praying, marching, standing, giving: a month of statewide solidarity sponsored with the mayors of Hartford, New Haven, Stamford, and Westport.

events.title: Praying Together to "Stop the Violence"
events.body: The National Faith Leaders Collaborative praying together, part of Connecticut's Season of Peace, Healing and Reconciliation.

events.title: Hartford HealthCare / St. Vincent's Opportunity Fair
events.body: Let's put people to work: the CT Faith Jobs Collaborative and Hartford HealthCare brought hiring to old Bridgeport City Hall, the model for the jobs pipeline this app now runs year-round.

events.title: "Shared Legacies" Screening with Senator Richard Blumenthal
events.body: Christian, Jewish, and ecumenical leaders hosted a screening on the history of civil-rights collaboration, part of the Season of Peace featuring SCLC President Dr. Charles Steele, Jr.

events.title: Martin Luther King, Jr. Corridors: Connecticut Launch
events.location: Spear Park, corner of MLK & Main Street, Middletown, CT
events.body: The public launch of the MLK Corridors Initiative: Norwalk, Middletown, and New Britain named as Connecticut's corridor communities.

events.title: 2nd Annual CT Community & Law Enforcement Engagement Summit
events.body: "Come, Let Us Reason… Together," with the Black & Latino Caucus, the Governor, Senator Blumenthal, police chiefs, mayors, the NAACP, the Urban League, and congregations statewide.

events.title: Southern Connecticut Housing and Development Summit
events.location: Margaret Morton Government Center, Bridgeport, CT
events.body: With FHLB–San Francisco, HUD's regional director, and Bridgeport's planning office, plus the introduction of "FREEDOM to Work," a new jobs program for Bridgeport residents.

events.title: 7th Annual Bridgeport Regional Sustainability Summit
events.location: University of Bridgeport & Shiloh Baptist Church
events.body: "Learning to breathe easier and build better": a full day of community training, a clergy luncheon, and an evening community conversation.
